raw_consumer_statement: I have been getting emails ( but not statements ) re : a balance with citi card. I have not used this card in years. In fact, I believed I closed the account years ago, so I assumed these were scam emails. I was not receiving a new card when the old one was expired. I never carried this card in my wallet. When I tried to set up an account to view my balance, it would say that cc # is invalid, which it isn't so I would give up. I first tried to call citi a couple years ago, and they wouldn't tell me what the balance on my card was from. I finally tried to resolve this again and called citi several times back in XXXX, and each time they were unable to get into my account. I was persistent called each day my husband was around ( they say his name was the Main cardholder yet MY mothers maiden name and my social security # was the primary on the acct and wouldn't talk to me unless my husband was present ) The first time I called to resolve this issue was mid XXXX. I have called them at LEAST a dozen times to resolve this, each time no one calls me back. There were monthly checks FROM my XXXX XXXX XXXX account going to Citi to pay a balance that was NOT my balance. So I stopped payment ( it was Autopay ) with my bank. Last time I called citi, I finally got someone who could get into my account, and told me " I '' took out a loan on this card back in XXXX of XXXX. I DID NOT take out a loan! She said she would call back in 3-5 days, and she has not! Today I received a call telling me my card was overdue and threatening to send me to collections!! I told her part of the story, she said they need to cancel my card, that the previous rep should've done that. There is a balance of about {$1600.00} that is NOT my debt, I pay my debts. Please help, I'm getting no where.
